# US-AL-005: Learning Entity Schema

## Problem
Brain's knowledge graph has entities for decisions, observations, suggestions, tasks, features, and questions -- but no entity for persistent behavioral rules. When Tomas Eriksson corrects an agent, the correction lives only in conversation history. There is no structured entity to store, query, version, or inject behavioral learnings into agent prompts.

## Who
- System (schema layer) | Database infrastructure | Needs a SCHEMAFULL table to persist learning records with lifecycle, provenance, and vector search

## Job Story Trace
- **Job 1, 2, 3, 4** (all jobs) -- this is the foundational schema enabling all learning flows.

## Solution
Create a `learning` SCHEMAFULL table following existing entity patterns (observation, suggestion) with fields for text, type, status, source, target_agents, workspace, embedding, provenance, and lifecycle timestamps. Include relation tables for evidence and supersession edges.

## Domain Examples

### 1: Happy Path -- Human-created constraint
Tomas creates a learning via the chat interface. The system creates a record:
- text: "Never use null for domain data values. Represent absence with omitted optional fields."
- learning_type: "constraint"
- status: "active"
- source: "human"
- target_agents: ["code_agent", "chat_agent"]
- workspace: workspace:brain-dev
- created_by: identity:tomas-eriksson
- created_at: 2026-03-13T10:30:00Z
- embedding: [0.12, -0.34, ...] (1536 dimensions)
- priority: "medium"

### 2: Edge Case -- Agent-suggested learning with evidence
Observer creates a suggestion:
- text: "When creating SurrealDB queries with KNN and WHERE, split into two steps."
- learning_type: "constraint"
- status: "pending_approval"
- source: "agent"
- suggested_by: "observer"
- confidence: 0.89
- target_agents: ["code_agent"]
- evidence_refs: [agent_session:a1b2, agent_session:c3d4, agent_session:e5f6]

### 3: Error/Boundary -- Superseded learning
Old learning "Use snake_case for DB fields" is superseded by new learning "Use camelCase for all SurrealDB field names":
- Old record: status changes to "superseded", superseded_by set to new learning ID
- New record: status "active", supersedes set to old learning ID
- Supersession chain preserved for history

## UAT Scenarios (BDD)

### Scenario: Learning table created with all required fields
Given the migration script is applied
When checking INFO FOR TABLE learning
Then the table is SCHEMAFULL
And contains fields: text (string), learning_type (string), status (string), source (string), target_agents (array of string), workspace (record of workspace), created_at (datetime), updated_at (option datetime), embedding (option array of float)

### Scenario: Human-created learning record persisted
Given Tomas creates a learning with text "Never use null" and type "constraint"
When the record is created in SurrealDB
Then the record has status "active" and source "human"
And created_by references Tomas's identity
And an embedding is generated and stored

### Scenario: Agent-suggested learning with evidence refs
Given the Observer creates a learning suggestion
When the record is created with source "agent" and suggested_by "observer"
Then status is "pending_approval"
And evidence_refs contains 3 agent_session record IDs
And confidence is stored as a float between 0 and 1

### Scenario: Status transition from pending_approval to active
Given a learning with status "pending_approval" exists
When Tomas approves it
Then status changes to "active"
And approved_by references Tomas's identity
And approved_at is set to current timestamp

### Scenario: Supersession preserves history
Given active learning A "Use snake_case" exists
When learning B "Use camelCase" supersedes it
Then learning A status changes to "superseded"
And learning A has superseded_by referencing learning B
And learning B has supersedes referencing learning A
And learning B status is "active"

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `learning` table is SCHEMAFULL with all fields defined and type-constrained
- [ ] `learning_type` asserted IN ["constraint", "instruction", "precedent"]
- [ ] `status` asserted IN ["active", "pending_approval", "dismissed", "superseded", "deactivated"]
- [ ] `source` asserted IN ["human", "agent"]
- [ ] `target_agents` is array of strings, each asserted IN ["code_agent", "chat_agent", "pm_agent", "architect", "observer", "design_partner"]
- [ ] Embedding field supports HNSW index for vector search (duplicate/conflict detection)
- [ ] Workspace, status, and created_at indexes defined for query performance
- [ ] Supersession fields (superseded_by, supersedes) support learning evolution
- [ ] Migration script is versioned and transactional

## Technical Notes
- Schema follows existing patterns: observation table (text, status, workspace, embedding, source_agent, created_at) and suggestion table (text, status, workspace, embedding, confidence, suggested_by)
- HNSW index on embedding field using DIMENSION 1536 DIST COSINE (same as observation, suggestion)
- Workspace index for feed queries: `DEFINE INDEX learning_workspace_status ON learning FIELDS workspace, status`
- Migration file: `schema/migrations/NNNN_add_learning_table.surql` (next available prefix)
- No data migration needed -- new table, no existing data
- Relation tables: `learning_evidence` TYPE RELATION for evidence edges, `supersedes` TYPE RELATION for supersession

## Dependencies
- No dependencies -- this is the foundation other stories depend on
- Enables: US-AL-001, US-AL-002, US-AL-003, US-AL-004

content.content: File created successfully at: /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/requirements/agent-learnings/US-AL-005-learning-schema.md

# US-AL-006: Learning Conflict Detection

## Problem
Tomas Eriksson's workspace has 12 active learnings accumulated over 2 months. Two of them contradict each other: "Never use null for domain data values" and "Return null from API endpoints when resource not found." Neither Tomas nor the agents noticed the conflict because there is no mechanism to detect semantic contradictions between active learnings. The coding agent receives both rules and behaves unpredictably.

## Who
- System (conflict detector) | Learning creation and curation | Needs to detect contradictions before they cause agent confusion
- Workspace owner | Managing learning library | Wants conflicts surfaced proactively

## Job Story Trace
- **Job 4**: Learning Governance and Curation
- **When** my workspace has accumulated 30+ learnings, **I want to** detect and resolve conflicts, **so I can** maintain a coherent set that improves rather than degrades agent behavior.

## Solution
Implement semantic conflict detection at two points: (1) on learning creation/approval (proactive), and (2) on the active learning set periodically (retrospective). Surface conflicts as observations or inline warnings with resolution options.

## Domain Examples

### 1: Happy Path -- Conflict detected at creation time
Tomas saves a new learning "Always return null for optional API response fields." The system computes an embedding and searches active learnings. It finds "Never use null for domain data values" with high semantic similarity but opposing sentiment. A conflict warning appears before save, showing both learnings and offering "Supersede existing," "Save both (clarify scopes)," or "Cancel."

### 2: Edge Case -- Non-conflicting similar learnings
Tomas saves "Use integer cents for billing calculations." The system finds "Use integer math for currency calculations" with similarity 0.82. These are complementary, not contradictory. The system shows a "Similar learning exists" notice (not a conflict warning) and offers to merge or save separately.

### 3: Error/Boundary -- Retrospective conflict in existing library
Two learnings were created weeks apart and never compared. The periodic scan detects that "Always use camelCase for variables" and "Use snake_case for database fields" have high similarity but are actually non-conflicting (different scopes). The system marks them as "reviewed, non-conflicting" after Tomas confirms.

## UAT Scenarios (BDD)

### Scenario: Conflict detected at learning creation
Given an active learning "Never use null for domain data values" exists
And its embedding is stored
When Tomas saves a new learning "Always return null for optional API response fields"
Then the system computes the new learning's embedding
And finds the existing learning with semantic similarity above 0.75
And detects opposing intent (contradiction analysis)
And shows a conflict warning with both learnings
And offers "Supersede existing", "Save both (clarify scopes)", "Cancel"

### Scenario: Duplicate detected (not conflict)
Given an active learning "Never persist null for domain data values" exists
When Tomas saves "Do not use null in domain data"
Then semantic similarity is above 0.85 with same intent
And a duplicate warning appears (not a conflict warning)
And offers "Update existing", "Save as separate", "Cancel"

### Scenario: Similar but non-conflicting learnings
Given an active learning "Use integer cents for billing" exists
When Tomas saves "Use integer math for currency calculations"
Then semantic similarity is above 0.75
But intent analysis shows complementary (not contradictory)
And an informational notice appears "Similar learning exists"
And both can be saved without conflict

### Scenario: Conflict resolution via supersession
Given a conflict is detected between learning A and new learning B
When Tomas chooses "Supersede existing"
Then learning A status changes to "superseded"
And learning A gains superseded_by referencing learning B
And learning B is saved with status "active"

### Scenario: Retrospective conflict scan
Given 12 active learnings exist in workspace "Brain Development"
When the periodic conflict scan runs
Then all active learning pairs are compared by embedding similarity
And pairs with similarity above 0.75 are analyzed for contradiction
And detected conflicts surface as observations in the governance feed

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] On learning creation: embedding similarity search against all active learnings in workspace
- [ ] Similarity above 0.85 with same intent: flagged as duplicate
- [ ] Similarity above 0.75 with opposing intent: flagged as conflict
- [ ] Conflict warning shows both learnings with resolution options (Supersede, Save Both, Cancel)
- [ ] Duplicate warning offers Update Existing, Save Separate, Cancel
- [ ] Supersession correctly transitions old learning to "superseded" status
- [ ] Periodic retrospective scan compares all active learning pairs

## Technical Notes
- Conflict detection uses the same embedding pipeline and KNN search as duplicate detection
- Intent analysis (same vs opposing) requires LLM call -- compare two learning texts to classify as "complementary," "contradictory," or "unrelated"
- Use the existing two-step KNN pattern for SurrealDB v3.0 (LET subquery + WHERE filter)
- Retrospective scan can run as part of Observer's existing graph scan loop
- Keep conflict threshold configurable (default 0.75 for conflicts, 0.85 for duplicates)

## Dependencies
- Depends on: US-AL-005 (Learning Schema) -- embeddings must exist for comparison
- Depends on: Embedding pipeline (existing)
- Optional enhancement for: US-AL-001 (validates at creation), US-AL-004 (surfaces in feed)

content.content: File created successfully at: /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/requirements/agent-learnings/US-AL-006-conflict-detection.md

# Peer Review: Agent Learnings Requirements Package

```yaml
review_id: "req_rev_20260313_001"
reviewer: "product-owner (review mode)"
artifact: "docs/requirements/agent-learnings/"
iteration: 1

strengths:
  - "All 6 stories trace to specific JTBD job stories with explicit job numbers"
  - "Real persona (Tomas Eriksson) with specific context used throughout -- no generic 'user' references"
  - "Domain examples use realistic data: actual SurrealDB conventions (null vs undefined, KNN bug, RecordId patterns) grounded in the codebase"
  - "Four Forces analysis identifies real adoption blockers (anxiety about permanence, habit of CLAUDE.md files)"
  - "Opportunity scoring provides evidence-based prioritization, not gut feel"
  - "Schema story (US-AL-005) correctly identifies existing entity patterns (observation, suggestion) as templates"
  - "Emotional arcs map naturally to the Problem Relief and Confidence Building patterns"
  - "Shared artifacts registry tracks 10 artifacts with sources, consumers, and integration risk levels"

issues_identified:
  confirmation_bias:
    - issue: "No consideration of alternative approaches to learning persistence (e.g., learning as a subtype of existing suggestion entity rather than new table)"
      severity: "medium"
      location: "US-AL-005"
      recommendation: "Add a brief note acknowledging the design choice (new table vs extending suggestion) and why -- this is acceptable as a DISCUSS wave artifact since DESIGN wave makes the final call"

  completeness_gaps:
    - issue: "Missing: learning deactivation flow. Stories mention 'deactivated' status but no UAT scenario covers how a user deactivates a learning."
      severity: "high"
      location: "US-AL-001, US-AL-004"
      recommendation: "Add scenario to US-AL-004: 'Given Tomas views the learning library When he clicks Deactivate on an active learning Then status changes to deactivated and learning is no longer injected.' This is within existing story scope."
    - issue: "Missing: learning library view as a standalone route. Journey visual shows it but no story owns the library page/component."
      severity: "high"
      location: "US-AL-004"
      recommendation: "US-AL-004 scope should explicitly include the learning library view (list with filters). Add AC: 'Learning library view accessible showing all learnings with filter by status, agent, type.' This keeps it one story since library is a simple filtered list."
    - issue: "Missing: how agents reference learnings in their output. Journey visual shows agent saying 'Following workspace convention...' but no AC covers this."
      severity: "medium"
      location: "US-AL-003"
      recommendation: "This is a nice-to-have, not core. Note it as a future enhancement rather than adding to current scope."

  clarity_issues:
    - issue: "Token budget of '~500 tokens' is vague. Should specify whether this is configurable or hardcoded."
      severity: "medium"
      location: "US-AL-003"
      recommendation: "Clarify: 'Token budget defaults to 500 tokens, configurable per workspace.' Add to technical notes."
    - issue: "Correction pattern detection threshold (3 occurrences in 14 days) -- unclear if these are from the same user or any user"
      severity: "medium"
      location: "US-AL-001, US-AL-002"
      recommendation: "Clarify: corrections from any user in the workspace count toward the threshold."

  testability_concerns:
    - issue: "Intent analysis for conflict detection (complementary vs contradictory) requires LLM call -- non-deterministic"
      severity: "medium"
      location: "US-AL-006"
      recommendation: "Tag intent analysis scenarios with @property to indicate they test a probabilistic quality. Add: 'Intent analysis correctly classifies at least 80% of known contradiction pairs.'"

  priority_validation:
    q1_largest_bottleneck: "YES"
    q2_simple_alternatives: "ADEQUATE"
    q3_constraint_prioritization: "CORRECT"
    q4_data_justified: "JUSTIFIED"
    verdict: "PASS"

approval_status: "conditionally_approved"
critical_issues_count: 0
high_issues_count: 2
```

1→# US-AL-004: Learning Governance Feed Cards
     2→
     3→## Problem
     4→Tomas Eriksson's workspace has accumulated 3 pending learning suggestions from the Observer and PM agents this week. He has no way to see them -- they exist in the database but do not surface in his governance feed. Without visibility, agent-suggested learnings pile up unreviewed, and the agents never benefit from the wisdom they detected.
     5→
     6→## Who
     7→- Workspace owner | Reviewing governance feed daily | Wants to efficiently approve, edit, or dismiss pending learnings
     8→
     9→## Job Story Trace
    10→- **Job 4**: Learning Governance and Curation
    11→- **When** agents have suggested behavioral learnings, **I want to** review them in my governance feed with evidence, **so I can** approve valuable rules and dismiss low-quality ones efficiently.
    12→
    13→## Solution
    14→Surface pending learning suggestions as yellow-tier governance feed cards with evidence display, confidence indicator, and Approve/Edit & Approve/Dismiss controls. Include a "Learnings" filter in the feed and provide a learning library view for curation of all learnings.
    15→
    16→## Domain Examples
    17→
    18→### 1: Happy Path -- Tomas approves a high-confidence suggestion
    19→Tomas opens the governance feed and sees a yellow card: "Never use null for domain data values," suggested by Observer with 89% confidence, targeting code_agent, with evidence from 3 sessions. He clicks "Approve." The learning transitions to "active" immediately, a confirmation appears, and the card disappears from the pending feed.
    20→
    21→### 2: Edge Case -- Tomas edits and approves a suggestion
    22→A pending suggestion reads "Avoid null in code." Tomas clicks "Edit & Approve," expands the text to "Never use null for domain data values. Represent absence with omitted optional fields (field?: Type) only. If null appears, treat as contract violation and fix the producer." He also adds chat_agent to the target agents. The refined learning is saved as active.
    23→
    24→### 3: Error/Boundary -- Tomas dismisses a low-confidence suggestion
    25→A suggestion "Prefer shorter task titles under 60 characters" has 62% confidence, suggested by PM agent. Tomas clicks "Dismiss," enters optional reason "too subjective, not a universal rule," and confirms. The learning is marked "dismissed" and removed from the feed. The dismissal reason is stored for future reference.
    26→
    27→## UAT Scenarios (BDD)
    28→
    29→### Scenario: Pending learning appears as yellow-tier feed card
    30→Given the Observer created a learning suggestion with status "pending_approval"
    31→And confidence is 0.89 and target_agents is ["code_agent"]
    32→When Tomas opens the governance feed
    33→Then a yellow-tier card appears showing the suggestion text
    34→And the card displays "Suggested by: Observer | Confidence: 89%"
    35→And the card displays "For: code_agent"
    36→And the card shows evidence from 3 linked sessions
    37→And buttons "Approve", "Edit & Approve", "Dismiss" are visible
    38→
    39→### Scenario: Approve transitions learning to active
    40→Given a pending learning suggestion is displayed in the feed
    41→When Tomas clicks "Approve"
    42→Then the learning status changes to "active"
    43→And approved_by references Tomas Eriksson
    44→And approved_at is set to current time
    45→And a confirmation "Learning approved and activated" appears
    46→And the card is removed from the pending feed
    47→
    48→### Scenario: Edit and approve with text refinement
    49→Given a pending learning suggestion is displayed
    50→When Tomas clicks "Edit & Approve"
    51→Then the learning editor opens with pre-filled text and settings
    52→When Tomas modifies the text and adds chat_agent to targets
    53→And clicks "Save & Activate"
    54→Then the learning is saved with edited text and status "active"
    55→
    56→### Scenario: Dismiss with reason
    57→Given a pending suggestion with confidence 0.62 is displayed
    58→When Tomas clicks "Dismiss"
    59→Then an optional reason input appears
    60→When Tomas enters "too subjective" and confirms
    61→Then the learning status changes to "dismissed"
    62→And dismissed_reason, dismissed_by, dismissed_at are recorded
    63→And the card is removed from the feed
    64→
    65→### Scenario: Feed filter for learnings
    66→Given 3 pending learning suggestions and 5 pending entity suggestions exist
    67→When Tomas selects the "Learnings" filter on the governance feed
    68→Then only the 3 learning suggestion cards are displayed
    69→
    70→### Scenario: Evidence drill-down
    71→Given a pending learning with 3 evidence_refs is displayed
    72→When Tomas expands the evidence section
    73→Then each evidence shows session date, correction quote, and session link
    74→
    75→## Acceptance Criteria
    76→- [ ] Pending learnings (status = "pending_approval") appear as yellow-tier governance feed cards
    77→- [ ] Feed cards display: suggestion text, suggested_by, confidence, target_agents, evidence quotes
    78→- [ ] "Approve" transitions status to "active" with approved_by and approved_at
    79→- [ ] "Edit & Approve" opens learning editor pre-filled with suggestion data, saves as active on confirm
    80→- [ ] "Dismiss" captures optional reason and transitions to "dismissed" with dismissed_by and dismissed_at
    81→- [ ] Governance feed has a "Learnings" filter tab
    82→- [ ] Evidence drill-down shows session dates, correction quotes, and links to session records
    83→
    84→## Technical Notes
    85→- Feed card rendering follows existing suggestion card pattern in `feed-queries.ts` and `feed-route.ts`
    86→- Learning status transitions follow the same actor-timestamped pattern as suggestion accept/dismiss
    87→- Feed query joins learning table WHERE status = "pending_approval" AND workspace = $workspace
    88→- Evidence rendering requires joining evidence_refs to agent_session records for date and correction text
    89→- Learning editor component is reused from US-AL-001 (human creation) with minor adaptation for pre-fill
    90→
    91→## Dependencies
    92→- Depends on: US-AL-005 (Learning Schema) -- table must exist
    93→- Depends on: US-AL-002 (Agent Suggests) -- suggestions must exist to display
    94→- Depends on: Existing feed infrastructure (feed-queries.ts, feed-route.ts)
    95→- Enables: US-AL-003 (Runtime Injection) -- approved learnings become injectable
    96→

input.content:
# DISCUSS Wave Handoff: Agent Learnings -> DESIGN Wave

## Status: Ready for Handoff
- DoR: All 6 stories PASSED (see dor-validation.md)
- Peer Review: Approved (iteration 2, see peer-review.md)
- All critical/high issues resolved

---

## Package Contents

### JTBD Analysis
| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `docs/ux/agent-learnings/jtbd-analysis.md` | 4 job stories, forces analysis, opportunity scoring, persona definitions, story bridge |

### Journey Artifacts (docs/ux/agent-learnings/)
| File | Journey |
|------|---------|
| `journey-human-creates-learning-visual.md` | Human correction flow with emotional arc, ASCII mockups, error paths |
| `journey-agent-suggests-learning-visual.md` | Agent suggestion + cross-agent coaching flow |
| `journey-governance-review-visual.md` | Feed review + learning library curation |
| `journey-runtime-injection-visual.md` | JIT prompt injection pipeline |
| `journey-human-creates-learning.yaml` | Structured schema: steps, artifacts, Gherkin, integration |
| `journey-agent-suggests-learning.yaml` | Structured schema: detection, suggestion, feed, approval |
| `journey-runtime-injection.yaml` | Structured schema: query, sort, budget, inject |
| `journey-human-creates-learning.feature` | 9 Gherkin scenarios |
| `journey-agent-suggests-learning.feature` | 11 Gherkin scenarios |
| `journey-runtime-injection.feature` | 11 Gherkin scenarios (incl. 2 @property) |
| `shared-artifacts-registry.md` | 10 tracked artifacts with sources, consumers, risk levels, integration checkpoints |

### Requirements (docs/requirements/agent-learnings/)
| File | Story | Priority | Effort |
|------|-------|----------|--------|
| `US-AL-005-learning-schema.md` | Learning Entity Schema | Must Have | 1-2 days |
| `US-AL-001-human-creates-learning.md` | Human Creates Learning | Must Have | 2-3 days |
| `US-AL-003-runtime-injection.md` | JIT Prompt Injection | Must Have | 2 days |
| `US-AL-004-governance-feed-cards.md` | Governance Feed + Library | Should Have | 2-3 days |
| `US-AL-002-agent-suggests-learning.md` | Agent Suggests Learning | Should Have | 2-3 days |
| `US-AL-006-conflict-detection.md` | Conflict Detection | Should Have | 2-3 days |
| `dor-validation.md` | DoR validation for all 6 stories | -- | -- |
| `peer-review.md` | Peer review (2 iterations, approved) | -- | -- |

---

## Recommended Implementation Order

```
Phase 1 (Foundation):
  US-AL-005 (Schema) ─────────────────────────────┐
                                                    │
Phase 2 (Core Value, parallel):                    │
  US-AL-001 (Human Creates) ──┐                    │
  US-AL-003 (Runtime Inject) ─┤── delivers MVP     │
                               │                    │
Phase 3 (Governance):         │                    │
  US-AL-004 (Feed + Library) ─┘                    │
                                                    │
Phase 4 (Self-Improvement):                        │
  US-AL-002 (Agent Suggests) ──────────────────────┘
                               │
Phase 5 (Safety):             │
  US-AL-006 (Conflict Detection) ─────────────────
```

Phase 1+2 deliver the core job: "say it once, agents remember forever."
Phase 3+4 deliver the self-improvement loop: agents suggest, humans approve.
Phase 5 is a safety net for accumulated learnings.

---

## Key Design Decisions for Solution Architect

These are solution-neutral requirements. The DESIGN wave makes technology choices. Key decisions needed:

1. **Learning table design**: New table (recommended, follows observation/suggestion pattern) vs extending suggestion table with a `kind` discriminator
2. **Correction detection mechanism**: How does the chat agent detect repeated corrections? Embedding similarity on recent messages? Explicit keyword patterns? Both?
3. **Token budget strategy**: Hard limit vs soft limit for human learnings. Exact tokenizer vs word-count heuristic.
4. **Conflict detection depth**: Embedding similarity alone vs embedding + LLM intent analysis for contradiction classification
5. **Observer integration**: New scan type in existing Observer loop vs separate scheduled job for learning pattern detection
6. **Feed card component**: Extend existing suggestion card vs new learning-specific card component

---

## Existing Codebase Integration Points

| Integration Point | File | What Changes |
|-------------------|------|-------------|
| Schema | `schema/migrations/NNNN_add_learning_table.surql` | New migration |
| Chat prompt | `app/src/server/chat/context.ts` | Add learnings to ChatContext, inject in buildSystemPrompt |
| PM prompt | `app/src/server/agents/pm/prompt.ts` | Add learnings section |
| MCP context | `cli/src/context-builder.ts` | Add learnings to context packet |
| Observer context | `app/src/server/agents/observer/context-loader.ts` | Add learnings section |
| Feed queries | `app/src/server/feed/feed-queries.ts` | Add learning feed items |
| Feed route | `app/src/server/feed/feed-route.ts` | Add learning endpoints |
| Shared contracts | `app/src/shared/contracts.ts` | Add LearningSummary type |
| Server routes | `app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts` | Register learning routes |

---

## Risk Assessment

| Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|------------|--------|------------|
| Prompt bloat from excessive learnings | Medium | Medium | Token budget with human-override. Observation alert on budget exceed. |
| Low-quality agent suggestions | Medium | Low | Human approval gate. Confidence threshold. Dismissal feedback loop. |
| Conflicting learnings confuse agents | Low | High | Conflict detection at creation. Priority ordering at injection. |
| Learning accumulation without curation | Medium | Medium | Library view with filters. Periodic conflict scan. |
| Performance impact on session startup | Low | Medium | Learning query is simple indexed lookup. Budget cap limits payload size. |

---

## Glossary (Ubiquitous Language)

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| Learning | A persistent behavioral rule stored in the knowledge graph |
| Active | A learning with status "active" -- injected into agent prompts |
| Pending | A learning with status "pending_approval" -- awaiting human review |
| Constraint | Learning type: must-follow rule ("never do X") |
| Instruction | Learning type: conditional guidance ("when Y, do Z") |
| Precedent | Learning type: historical reference ("in the past, we did A") |
| Superseded | A learning replaced by a newer learning -- preserved in history |
| Deactivated | A learning manually turned off -- not injected but visible in library |
| JIT Prompting | Just-In-Time injection of learnings into system prompts at session start |
| Cross-Agent Coaching | One agent suggesting a learning for a different agent type |
| Learning Library | Curation view showing all learnings with filters and management controls |

IMPORTANT: If this was a trivial session (greetings, test messages, simple Q&A with no implementation work), immediately respond with:

Otherwise, check each category:

1. **Decisions**: Architecture or design choices where alternatives existed (e.g. choosing a pattern, library, data model, or trade-off). Do NOT log mechanical bugfixes, typo corrections, or changes with only one correct approach. Call create_provisional_decision for each genuine decision.

content.text: Great question. Let me check what the observer agent currently scans and how traces are structured.

input.prompt:
Explore /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi thoroughly (medium-high):

1. **Observer agent implementation**: Find all files in app/src/server/observer/ — read the main agent file, context loader, prompt, tools, and any graph scanning logic. Understand what data sources the observer currently scans.

2. **Trace table schema**: Search schema/surreal-schema.surql and schema/migrations/ for any "trace" table definition. What fields does it have? What does it capture?

3. **Agent session schema**: Search for "agent_session" in the schema. What's recorded per session?

4. **How observations are currently created by the observer**: What triggers the observer? What data does it look at to generate observations?

5. **Existing graph scanning queries**: Look at observer queries — what tables/relations does it traverse?

6. **Tool call recording**: Is there any mechanism that records what tools agents used, what decisions they made, what errors occurred?

Return a structured answer about:
- What data sources the observer currently has access to
- Whether traces exist and what they capture

Excellent! Now I have the full picture. Let me compile a comprehensive structured report:

## Observer Agent Implementation & Tracing/Learning Infrastructure Analysis

### 1. OBSERVER AGENT DATA SOURCES

The observer has access to the following data sources:

**Graph Scan Sources** (periodic workspace-wide analysis):
- **Confirmed Decisions** (up to 50 most recent, by creation date)
  - Queries: `SELECT id, summary, rationale FROM decision WHERE status = "confirmed"`
  - Data captured: decision summary, rationale, creation timestamp
  
- **Completed Tasks** (up to 50 most recent, by update date)
  - Queries: `SELECT id, title, description FROM task WHERE status IN ["completed", "done"]`
  - Data captured: task title, description, completion timestamp
  
- **Stale Blocked Tasks** (blocked >14 days)
  - Queries: `SELECT id, title, description, updated_at FROM task WHERE status = "blocked" AND updated_at < $threshold`
  - Data captured: how long blocked (days), description, last update time
  
- **Status Drift Tasks** (completed tasks with incomplete dependencies)
  - Queries: Multi-hop graph traversal: `->depends_on->task` to find blocking relationships
  - Data captured: dependency chains, status mismatches
  
- **Related Decisions** (per-entity context, up to 20 per project)
  - Queries: `SELECT id, summary, status, rationale FROM decision WHERE status IN ["confirmed", "provisional"]`
  - Data captured: related decisions in the same project as the entity being verified

**External Signals** (real-time):
- **Git Commit Status** (from GitHub API)
  - Fetches CI/build status for linked commits via GitHub API
  - Caches: sha, repository, ciStatus ("success"|"failure"|"pending"|"unknown")

**Entity Context Loader** (when verifying specific entities):
- Loads entity title, description, status
- Resolves entity's project via `belongs_to` edge
- Loads related decisions in that project

---

### 2. TRACE TABLE SCHEMA

**Location**: `schema/migrations/0023_trace_table.surql`

**Fields Captured**:
```
- id (RecordId<trace>)
- type (string): "tool_call" | "message" | "subagent_spawn" | "intent_submission" | "bridge_exchange"
- actor (record<identity>)
- workspace (record<workspace>)
- session (option<record<agent_session>>)
- parent_trace (option<record<trace>>)  // hierarchical tree
- tool_name (option<string>)             // what tool was called
- input (option<object FLEXIBLE>)        // tool arguments
- output (option<object FLEXIBLE>)       // tool result
- duration_ms (option<int>)              // execution time
- created_at (datetime)
- Indexes: parent_trace, session, workspace, created_at
```

**Trace Hierarchy**: Traces form a parent-child tree via `parent_trace` field, enabling full execution call stacks.

**Wire Format**: Traces are persisted atomically in a transaction:
- Root trace: represents a subagent spawn (e.g., `invoke_pm_agent`)
- Child traces: each represents a tool call or message step within the agent
- Edge: `spawns` relation links message → root trace

**What's NOT Captured**:
- Tool call failures/errors are NOT explicitly recorded as trace fields
- Agent decision rationale is NOT stored in traces
- Tool call latency metrics beyond `duration_ms`
- Retry attempts or backoff patterns

---

### 3. AGENT SESSION SCHEMA

**Location**: `schema/migrations/0001_agent_session_and_subtask.surql`

**Fields Captured**:
```
- id (RecordId<agent_session>)
- agent (string)                         // which agent (coding, PM, etc.)
- started_at (datetime)
- ended_at (option<datetime>)
- workspace (record<workspace>)
- project (record<project>)
- task_id (option<record<task>>)         // which task was being worked on
- summary (option<string>)               // session summary
- decisions_made (option<array<record<decision>>>)
- questions_asked (option<array<record<question>>>)
- tasks_progressed (option<array<object>>)  // with from_status → to_status
  - task_id, from_status, to_status
- files_changed (option<array<object>>)  // code changes
  - path, change_type ("created"|"modified"|"deleted")
- created_at (datetime)
- Indexes: workspace, project, started_at
```

**Relations**:
- `produced`: agent_session → decision
- `asked`: agent_session → question
- `progressed`: agent_session → task (with status transition)

**What's NOT Captured**:
- Tool calls made during the session (those go in `trace` table separately)
- Error/failure reasons
- Agent reasoning or decision criteria
- Confidence scores for decisions made
- Resource consumption (tokens, API calls, cost)

---

### 4. HOW OBSERVATIONS ARE CURRENTLY CREATED

**Three Observation Types**:

1. **Contradiction Detection** (LLM-based graph scan)
   - Triggers: `runGraphScan()` compares confirmed decisions vs completed tasks
   - LLM Call: `detectContradictions()` analyzes pairs for mismatches
   - Observation created when: Task implements approach that violates confirmed decision
   - Severity: `conflict`
   - Data sources: decision summary, task title/description

2. **Anomaly Detection** (Stale/Drift, LLM-filtered)
   - Triggers: `queryStaleBlockedTasks()` (>14 days blocked) + `queryStatusDriftTasks()` (completed but dependencies incomplete)
   - LLM Filter: `evaluateAnomalies()` reduces false positives
   - Observation created when: LLM marks as "relevant" (not expected external dependency)
   - Severity: LLM-determined ("info"|"warning"|"conflict")
   - Data sources: task title, days blocked, dependency chain

3. **Pattern Synthesis** (LLM-based cross-cutting signals)
   - Triggers: `synthesizePatterns()` across all contradictions + anomalies
   - Patterns: `bottleneck_decision`, `cascade_block`, `priority_drift`, `stale_cluster`, `contradiction_cluster`
   - Observation created when: 2+ contributing entities form a pattern
   - Severity: LLM-determined ("warning"|"conflict")
   - Data sources: aggregated anomalies across workspace

**Deduplication**:
- Entity-level: `queryExistingObserverObservationsForEntity()` checks if entity already has open observation
- Text-level: `isAlreadyObserved()` uses string prefix matching (80-char threshold)

---

### 5. EXISTING GRAPH SCANNING QUERIES

Current queries in `graph-scan.ts`:

| Query | Data | Purpose |
|-------|------|---------|
| `queryConfirmedDecisions()` | id, summary, rationale, created_at | Find all confirmed decisions (limit 50) |
| `queryCompletedTasks()` | id, title, description, updated_at | Find completed/done tasks (limit 50) |
| `queryStaleBlockedTasks()` | id, title, description, updated_at | Find tasks blocked >14 days (limit 50) |
| `queryStatusDriftTasks()` | id, title, status, dep_ids, dep_titles, dep_statuses | Find completed tasks with incomplete deps |
| `queryExistingObserverObservationsForEntity()` | text, severity, status | Dedup: check if entity has open observation |

**Graph Traversals**:
- `->belongs_to->project`: entity → project
- `->depends_on->task`: task → blocking dependencies
- `<-observes<-observation`: entity ← observations about it

---

### 6. TOOL CALL RECORDING MECHANISM

**Location**: `app/src/server/chat/trace-loader.ts` + `observer-route.ts` webhooks

**Recording Flow**:
1. **Subagent Spawn**: `persistSubagentTrace()` creates root trace record
2. **Child Traces**: Each tool call becomes a child trace (type="tool_call")
3. **Atomicity**: All persisted in single transaction
4. **Access**: `batchLoadTraces()` reconstructs trace trees for chat UI/analysis

**What's Recorded**:
- ✅ Tool name called
- ✅ Input arguments (JSON)
- ✅ Output result (JSON)
- ✅ Execution duration (ms)
- ❌ Error messages/stack traces (not captured)
- ❌ Retry attempts (not tracked)
- ❌ Tool failure reasons (not structured)

---

### 7. KEY GAPS: DATA MISSING FOR PATTERN DETECTION & LEARNING

| Signal Type | Current Status | Gap |
|-------------|---|---|
| **Tool Failures** | Logged in observability only | Not stored in graph; can't query "which tools fail together" |
| **Repeated Mistakes** | No mechanism | Traces exist, but no pattern detection across sessions |
| **Tool Call Timing** | `duration_ms` only | No latency thresholds, timeout patterns, or performance regressions |
| **Error Categories** | Logged text | Not structured; can't query "type of errors" |
| **Decision Rationale** | Agent reasoning during session | Not persisted; only final decision stored |
| **Confidence Levels** | LLM verdicts (0.0-1.0) | Only for observations, not for agent decisions during sessions |
| **Learning Records** | Spec'd in US-AL-005 | **NOT YET IMPLEMENTED** in schema migrations |
| **Evidence Linking** | Suggested for learnings | No mechanism to link traces/sessions to learning suggestions |
| **Agent Performance Metrics** | None | No tracking of success rates, retry patterns, or correctness by agent/tool |
| **Cross-Agent Conflicts** | Not scanned | No detection of contradictions between different agents' outputs |

---

### 8. LEARNING TABLE STATUS

**Documentation**: `/docs/requirements/agent-learnings/US-AL-005-learning-schema.md`

**Spec'd Fields** (NOT YET IMPLEMENTED):
- `text` (string): constraint/instruction rule
- `learning_type` ("constraint"|"instruction"|"precedent")
- `status` ("active"|"pending_approval"|"dismissed"|"superseded"|"deactivated")
- `source` ("human"|"agent")
- `target_agents` (array<string>): ["code_agent", "chat_agent", "pm_agent", etc.]
- `workspace` (record<workspace>)
- `created_by`, `approved_by` (record<identity>)
- `embedding` (array<float>): for vector search / conflict detection
- `confidence` (float, 0-1): for agent-suggested learnings
- `evidence_refs` (array<record<agent_session|trace>>): proof of the learning
- `superseded_by`, `supersedes` (record<learning>): learning evolution

**Missing Migration File**: No `003X_add_learning_table.surql` found in `/schema/migrations/`

**Blocker**: Learning table is a **prerequisite** for storing repeated-mistake patterns, agent-suggested constraints, and building behavioral rules into prompts. All learning-related user stories (US-AL-001 through US-AL-004) depend on this schema.

---

### SUMMARY: What the Observer Currently Can Learn vs. What It Can't

**Can Learn** (via graph scan + LLM):
- Contradictions between confirmed decisions and completed task implementations
- Stale/blocked task patterns
- Status drift (completed tasks with incomplete dependencies)
- Cross-cutting patterns (bottlenecks, cascading blocks, priority drift)

**Cannot Learn** (no data structure):
- Tool failure patterns ("this tool fails 40% of the time when X is true")
- Repeated agent mistakes across sessions
- Error categories and recovery strategies
- Confidence thresholds that predict agent accuracy
- Agent-specific performance degradation over time
- Behavioral rules extracted from corrections (no learning table)

**Blocked**: Full learning system depends on implementing the `learning` table schema (US-AL-005).

---

## File Paths for Reference

**Observer Implementation**:
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/app/src/server/agents/observer/agent.ts`
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/app/src/server/agents/observer/prompt.ts`

- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/app/src/server/observer/external-signals.ts` (CI status)
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/app/src/server/observer/llm-synthesis.ts` (LLM reasoning)
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/app/src/server/observer/schemas.ts` (Zod output schemas)

**Trace & Session**:
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/app/src/server/chat/trace-loader.ts` (trace persistence/loading)
- Schema: `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/schema/migrations/0023_trace_table.surql`

The observer currently has **two data sources** for detecting learning-worthy patterns:

### What exists today

**1. Graph scan** (`observer/graph-scan.ts`) — periodic workspace-wide analysis:
- Confirmed decisions vs completed tasks → contradiction detection
- Stale blocked tasks (>14 days) → anomaly detection
- Status drift (completed tasks with incomplete dependencies)
- Cross-cutting pattern synthesis (bottleneck clusters, cascade blocks)

**2. Trace table** (`schema/migrations/0023_trace_table.surql`) — hierarchical execution trees:
- Tool calls with name, input, output, duration_ms
- Parent-child trace trees (subagent spawns → child tool calls)
- Linked to agent_session and workspace

### What's missing for learning suggestions

The observer **could** scan traces today to detect patterns like "this tool keeps getting called with bad arguments" — but it **doesn't yet**. The graph scan queries only look at decisions, tasks, and their relationships. No trace-level analysis exists.

Key gaps:

| Signal | Status |
|--------|--------|
| Tool failure patterns | Traces exist but errors aren't structured — only success output stored |
| Repeated mistakes across sessions | Agent sessions exist but no cross-session pattern detection |
| Correction patterns ("user said X, agent did Y anyway") | Not captured |
| Tool performance degradation | `duration_ms` exists but no threshold/trend analysis |

### Where learning data would actually come from

For agent-suggested learnings, there are **three realistic sources**:

1. **Traces** — observer scans tool call patterns across sessions (requires adding error capture to traces)
2. **Conversation history** — agent detects user corrections in chat messages ("no, don't do that", "I told you to...") and proposes a learning
3. **Observation escalation** — recurring observations of the same type get promoted to learning suggestions ("I've flagged this contradiction 3 times, this should be a rule")

Source #2 (conversation-based correction detection) is probably the highest-value, lowest-cost path — the chat agent already sees user frustration in real-time and could call `suggest_learning` immediately.

Want me to update the requirements to reflect these data source decisions, or proceed to `/nw:design`?

content.content:
1→/**
     2→ * Observer agent system prompt builder.
     3→ *
     4→ * OBSERVER_IDENTITY — static domain knowledge used by both the agent loop
     5→ * and the generateObject calls in llm-reasoning.ts.
     6→ *
     7→ * buildObserverSystemPrompt — async builder that composes identity + dynamic
     8→ * workspace context (existing observations) for agent loop invocations.
     9→ */
    10→
    11→import { RecordId, Surreal } from "surrealdb";
    12→import { listWorkspaceOpenObservations } from "../../observation/queries";
    13→
    14→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    15→// Static identity — imported by llm-reasoning.ts for generateObject system prompt
    16→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    17→
    18→export const OBSERVER_IDENTITY = `You are the Observer agent in Brain, the operating system for autonomous organizations.
    19→
    20→Brain is a knowledge graph where projects, decisions, tasks, features, observations, and questions are nodes. Agents (PM, Architect, Coding, Observer) coordinate through the graph — not by messaging each other.
    21→
    22→## Your Role
    23→You scan the graph for contradictions, risks, and drift. You create observations that surface signals to humans and other agents.
    24→
    25→## Key Domain Concepts
    26→- **Decision**: A confirmed organizational choice (e.g., "use tRPC for all APIs"). Decisions have status: extracted → proposed → provisional → confirmed → superseded.
    27→- **Task**: A unit of work. Tasks belong to projects and should comply with all confirmed decisions in their project.
    28→- **Observation**: A signal you create when you detect a contradiction, risk, or pattern. Severity: conflict (contradiction), warning (risk), info (awareness).
    29→- **belongs_to**: Graph edge linking tasks and decisions to their project.
    30→- **observes**: Graph edge linking an observation to the entities it concerns.
    31→
    32→## Verification Principles
    33→- A task that does something a confirmed decision explicitly forbids is a "mismatch" (contradiction).
    34→- A task that follows all relevant decisions is a "match".
    35→- When decisions don't address what the task does, or evidence is ambiguous, the result is "inconclusive".
    36→- Be decisive: if the text clearly shows a contradiction, say mismatch with high confidence. Reserve inconclusive for genuinely unclear cases.
    37→
    38→## Observation Rules
    39→- Create observations for: contradictions between decisions and implementations, stale blocked tasks, status drift, cross-project conflicts.
    40→- Set severity to conflict for contradictions, warning for risks, info for awareness.
    41→- Do NOT duplicate existing observations.
    42→- Always link observations to the entity they concern.`;
    43→
    44→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    45→// Dynamic workspace context builder
    46→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    47→
    48→function formatObservations(rows: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof listWorkspaceOpenObservations>>): string {
    49→  if (rows.length === 0) {
    50→    return "- none";
    51→  }
    52→
    53→  return rows
    54→    .slice(0, 30)
    55→    .map((row) => {
    56→      const category = row.category ? `, ${row.category}` : "";
    57→      return `- [${row.severity}] ${row.text} (${row.status}, by ${row.sourceAgent}${category})`;
    58→    })
    59→    .join("\n");
    60→}
    61→
    62→/**
    63→ * Composes OBSERVER_IDENTITY + dynamic workspace observations.
    64→ * Used by agent loop invocations (agent.ts). The generateObject calls
    65→ * in llm-reasoning.ts use OBSERVER_IDENTITY directly (no workspace context needed
    66→ * since entity context is provided in the user prompt).
    67→ */
    68→export async function buildObserverSystemPrompt(input: {
    69→  surreal: Surreal;
    70→  workspaceRecord: RecordId<"workspace", string>;
    71→}): Promise<string> {
    72→  const observations = await listWorkspaceOpenObservations({
    73→    surreal: input.surreal,
    74→    workspaceRecord: input.workspaceRecord,
    75→    limit: 30,
    76→  });
    77→
    78→  return [
    79→    OBSERVER_IDENTITY,
    80→    "",
    81→    "## Existing Workspace Observations",
    82→    formatObservations(observations),
    83→  ].join("\n");
    84→}
    85→

1→/**
     2→ * Zod schemas for LLM structured output in observer reasoning pipelines.
     3→ *
     4→ * Pure module — no IO imports. Schemas constrain generateObject JSON output.
     5→ */
     6→
     7→import { z } from "zod";
     8→
     9→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    10→// Verification verdict (semantic verification pipeline)
    11→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    12→
    13→export const llmVerdictSchema = z.object({
    14→  verdict: z.enum(["match", "mismatch", "inconclusive"]).describe(
    15→    "match = entity aligns with decisions, mismatch = contradiction detected, inconclusive = insufficient evidence",
    16→  ),
    17→  confidence: z.number().min(0).max(1).describe(
    18→    "Confidence in the verdict (0.0-1.0). Use <0.5 when evidence is ambiguous.",
    19→  ),
    20→  reasoning: z.string().describe(
    21→    "Natural language explanation of the verdict. Reference specific entities and decisions.",
    22→  ),
    23→  evidence_refs: z.array(z.string()).describe(
    24→    "Entity references in table:id format (e.g. decision:uuid, task:uuid) that support the verdict. Only include entities you were given in context.",
    25→  ),
    26→  contradiction: z.object({
    27→    claim: z.string().describe("What the entity claims or implements. Use 'none' when verdict is not mismatch."),
    28→    reality: z.string().describe("What the decision or constraint requires. Use 'none' when verdict is not mismatch."),
    29→  }).describe(
    30→    "Describes the specific contradiction. Set both fields to 'none' when verdict is not mismatch.",
    31→  ),
    32→});
    33→
    34→/** Parsed verdict with contradiction normalized to undefined when not a mismatch. */
    35→export type LlmVerdict = Omit<z.infer<typeof llmVerdictSchema>, "contradiction"> & {
    36→  contradiction?: { claim: string; reality: string };
    37→};
    38→
    39→/** Strip "none" sentinel from contradiction field after parsing. */
    40→export function parseLlmVerdict(raw: z.infer<typeof llmVerdictSchema>): LlmVerdict {
    41→  const { contradiction, ...rest } = raw;
    42→  const isNone = contradiction.claim === "none" && contradiction.reality === "none";
    43→  return isNone ? rest : { ...rest, contradiction };
    44→}
    45→
    46→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    47→// Synthesis pattern (pattern synthesis pipeline)
    48→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    49→
    50→export const synthesisPatternSchema = z.object({
    51→  pattern_name: z.enum([
    52→    "bottleneck_decision",
    53→    "cascade_block",
    54→    "priority_drift",
    55→    "stale_cluster",
    56→    "contradiction_cluster",
    57→  ]).describe(
    58→    "bottleneck_decision = single decision blocking multiple tasks, cascade_block = chain of blocked items, priority_drift = priorities inconsistent with actions, stale_cluster = group of stale items in same area, contradiction_cluster = multiple contradictions sharing a root cause",
    59→  ),
    60→  description: z.string().describe(
    61→    "Natural language synthesis explaining the pattern and its impact.",
    62→  ),
    63→  contributing_entities: z.array(z.string()).min(2).describe(
    64→    "Entity references in table:id format that contribute to this pattern. Minimum 2 required.",
    65→  ),
    66→  severity: z.enum(["warning", "conflict"]).describe(
    67→    "warning = potential risk, conflict = active contradiction requiring resolution",
    68→  ),
    69→  suggested_action: z.string().describe(
    70→    "Recommended action to address the pattern.",
    71→  ),
    72→});
    73→
    74→export type SynthesisPattern = z.infer<typeof synthesisPatternSchema>;
    75→
    76→export const synthesisResultSchema = z.object({
    77→  patterns: z.array(synthesisPatternSchema),
    78→});
    79→
    80→export type SynthesisResult = z.infer<typeof synthesisResultSchema>;
    81→
    82→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    83→// Contradiction detection (graph scan pipeline)
    84→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    85→
    86→export const detectedContradictionSchema = z.object({
    87→  decision_ref: z.string().describe(
    88→    "The contradicted decision in table:id format (e.g. decision:uuid). Must match an ID from the provided decisions.",
    89→  ),
    90→  task_ref: z.string().describe(
    91→    "The contradicting task in table:id format (e.g. task:uuid). Must match an ID from the provided tasks.",
    92→  ),
    93→  reasoning: z.string().describe(
    94→    "Concise explanation of why the task contradicts the decision.",
    95→  ),
    96→});
    97→
    98→export type DetectedContradiction = z.infer<typeof detectedContradictionSchema>;
    99→
   100→export const contradictionDetectionResultSchema = z.object({
   101→  contradictions: z.array(detectedContradictionSchema).describe(
   102→    "Pairs of (decision, task) where a completed task implements an approach that contradicts a confirmed decision. Only include clear contradictions, not minor differences.",
   103→  ),
   104→});
   105→
   106→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   107→// Peer review verdict (peer review pipeline)
   108→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   109→
   110→export const peerReviewVerdictSchema = z.object({
   111→  verdict: z.enum(["sound", "questionable", "unsupported"]).describe(
   112→    "sound = observation is well-grounded (>=0.7), questionable = partially supported (0.4-0.7), unsupported = lacks evidence (<0.4)",
   113→  ),
   114→  confidence: z.number().min(0).max(1).describe(
   115→    "Confidence in the review verdict (0.0-1.0).",
   116→  ),
   117→  reasoning: z.string().describe(
   118→    "Evidence evaluation explaining why the observation is or isn't well-grounded.",
   119→  ),
   120→});
   121→
   122→export type PeerReviewVerdict = z.infer<typeof peerReviewVerdictSchema>;
   123→
   124→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   125→// Anomaly evaluation verdict (stale/drift LLM reasoning)
   126→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   127→
   128→export const anomalyEvaluationSchema = z.object({
   129→  entity_ref: z.string().describe(
   130→    "The entity in table:id format being evaluated. Must match an entity from the provided list.",
   131→  ),
   132→  relevant: z.boolean().describe(
   133→    "true if this anomaly is genuinely concerning and warrants human attention. false if it is likely expected state, a known external dependency, or otherwise a false positive.",
   134→  ),
   135→  reasoning: z.string().describe(
   136→    "Brief explanation of why this anomaly is or is not relevant. Reference the task title and any contextual clues from the description.",
   137→  ),
   138→  suggested_severity: z.enum(["info", "warning", "conflict"]).describe(
   139→    "info = low concern (expected wait, external dependency). warning = genuine staleness or drift needing review. conflict = actively blocking critical work or contradicting decisions.",
   140→  ),
   141→});
   142→
   143→export type AnomalyEvaluation = z.infer<typeof anomalyEvaluationSchema>;
   144→
   145→export const anomalyEvaluationResultSchema = z.object({
   146→  evaluations: z.array(anomalyEvaluationSchema),
   147→});
   148→
   149→export type AnomalyEvaluationResult = z.infer<typeof anomalyEvaluationResultSchema>;
   150→

1→-- Observer agent schema extensions: add verified, source, data fields
     2→-- and extend observation_type enum with "validation" and "error".
     3→
     4→BEGIN TRANSACTION;
     5→
     6→-- New fields on observation table
     7→DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE verified ON observation TYPE bool DEFAULT false;
     8→DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE source ON observation TYPE option<string>;
     9→DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE data ON observation TYPE option<object> FLEXIBLE;
    10→
    11→-- Extend observation_type enum to include "validation" and "error"
    12→DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE observation_type ON observation TYPE option<string>
    13→  ASSERT $value IN ["contradiction", "duplication", "missing", "deprecated", "pattern", "anomaly", "validation", "error"] OR $value IS NONE;
    14→
    15→-- Extend observes relation OUT types to include intent, git_commit, observation
    16→DEFINE TABLE OVERWRITE observes TYPE RELATION IN observation OUT project | feature | task | decision | question | intent | git_commit | observation SCHEMAFULL;
    17→DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE added_at ON observes TYPE option<datetime>;
    18→
    19→COMMIT TRANSACTION;
    20→
    21→-- Observer agent SurrealDB EVENTs (outside transaction — ASYNC EVENTs may not
    22→-- work inside transactions in all SurrealDB versions).
    23→-- The base URL is a placeholder; wireObserverEvents() overwrites in tests,
    24→-- and production deploys configure via environment-specific migration.
    25→
    26→-- EVENT 1: task_completed — fires when task transitions to completed/done
    27→DEFINE EVENT OVERWRITE task_completed ON task
    28→  ASYNC
    29→  RETRY 3
    30→  WHEN $event = "UPDATE"
    31→    AND ($before.status != "completed" AND $before.status != "done")
    32→    AND ($after.status = "completed" OR $after.status = "done")
    33→  THEN {
    34→    http::post("http://localhost:3000/api/observe/task/" + <string> meta::id($after.id), $after)
    35→  };
    36→
    37→-- EVENT 2: intent_completed — fires when intent transitions to completed/failed
    38→DEFINE EVENT OVERWRITE intent_completed ON intent
    39→  ASYNC
    40→  RETRY 3
    41→  WHEN $event = "UPDATE"
    42→    AND ($before.status != "completed" AND $before.status != "failed")
    43→    AND ($after.status = "completed" OR $after.status = "failed")
    44→  THEN {
    45→    http::post("http://localhost:3000/api/observe/intent/" + <string> meta::id($after.id), $after)
    46→  };
    47→
    48→-- EVENT 3: commit_created — fires on new git_commit creation
    49→DEFINE EVENT OVERWRITE commit_created ON git_commit
    50→  ASYNC
    51→  RETRY 3
    52→  WHEN $event = "CREATE"
    53→  THEN {
    54→    http::post("http://localhost:3000/api/observe/git_commit/" + <string> meta::id($after.id), $after)
    55→  };
    56→
    57→-- EVENT 4: decision_confirmed — fires when decision transitions to confirmed/superseded
    58→DEFINE EVENT OVERWRITE decision_confirmed ON decision
    59→  ASYNC
    60→  RETRY 3
    61→  WHEN $event = "UPDATE"
    62→    AND $before.status != $after.status
    63→    AND ($after.status = "confirmed" OR $after.status = "superseded")
    64→  THEN {
    65→    http::post("http://localhost:3000/api/observe/decision/" + <string> meta::id($after.id), $after)
    66→  };
    67→
    68→-- EVENT 5: observation_peer_review — fires on non-observer observation creation
    69→-- Excludes observer_agent to prevent infinite recursion loops.
    70→DEFINE EVENT OVERWRITE observation_peer_review ON observation
    71→  ASYNC
    72→  RETRY 3
    73→  WHEN $event = "CREATE" AND $after.source_agent != "observer_agent"
    74→  THEN {
    75→    http::post("http://localhost:3000/api/observe/observation/" + <string> meta::id($after.id), $after)
    76→  };
    77→

1→# US-AL-005: Learning Entity Schema
     2→
     3→## Problem
     4→Brain's knowledge graph has entities for decisions, observations, suggestions, tasks, features, and questions -- but no entity for persistent behavioral rules. When Tomas Eriksson corrects an agent, the correction lives only in conversation history. There is no structured entity to store, query, version, or inject behavioral learnings into agent prompts.
     5→
     6→## Who
     7→- System (schema layer) | Database infrastructure | Needs a SCHEMAFULL table to persist learning records with lifecycle, provenance, and vector search
     8→
     9→## Job Story Trace
    10→- **Job 1, 2, 3, 4** (all jobs) -- this is the foundational schema enabling all learning flows.
    11→
    12→## Solution
    13→Create a `learning` SCHEMAFULL table following existing entity patterns (observation, suggestion) with fields for text, type, status, source, target_agents, workspace, embedding, provenance, and lifecycle timestamps. Include relation tables for evidence and supersession edges.
    14→
    15→## Domain Examples
    16→
    17→### 1: Happy Path -- Human-created constraint
    18→Tomas creates a learning via the chat interface. The system creates a record:
    19→- text: "Never use null for domain data values. Represent absence with omitted optional fields."
    20→- learning_type: "constraint"
    21→- status: "active"
    22→- source: "human"
    23→- target_agents: ["code_agent", "chat_agent"]
    24→- workspace: workspace:brain-dev
    25→- created_by: identity:tomas-eriksson
    26→- created_at: 2026-03-13T10:30:00Z
    27→- embedding: [0.12, -0.34, ...] (1536 dimensions)
    28→- priority: "medium"
    29→
    30→### 2: Edge Case -- Agent-suggested learning with evidence
    31→Observer creates a suggestion:
    32→- text: "When creating SurrealDB queries with KNN and WHERE, split into two steps."
    33→- learning_type: "constraint"
    34→- status: "pending_approval"
    35→- source: "agent"
    36→- suggested_by: "observer"
    37→- confidence: 0.89
    38→- target_agents: ["code_agent"]
    39→- evidence_refs: [agent_session:a1b2, agent_session:c3d4, agent_session:e5f6]
    40→
    41→### 3: Error/Boundary -- Superseded learning
    42→Old learning "Use snake_case for DB fields" is superseded by new learning "Use camelCase for all SurrealDB field names":
    43→- Old record: status changes to "superseded", superseded_by set to new learning ID
    44→- New record: status "active", supersedes set to old learning ID
    45→- Supersession chain preserved for history
    46→
    47→## UAT Scenarios (BDD)
    48→
    49→### Scenario: Learning table created with all required fields
    50→Given the migration script is applied
    51→When checking INFO FOR TABLE learning
    52→Then the table is SCHEMAFULL
    53→And contains fields: text (string), learning_type (string), status (string), source (string), target_agents (array of string), workspace (record of workspace), created_at (datetime), updated_at (option datetime), embedding (option array of float)
    54→
    55→### Scenario: Human-created learning record persisted
    56→Given Tomas creates a learning with text "Never use null" and type "constraint"
    57→When the record is created in SurrealDB
    58→Then the record has status "active" and source "human"
    59→And created_by references Tomas's identity
    60→And an embedding is generated and stored
    61→
    62→### Scenario: Agent-suggested learning with evidence refs
    63→Given the Observer creates a learning suggestion
    64→When the record is created with source "agent" and suggested_by "observer"
    65→Then status is "pending_approval"
    66→And evidence_refs contains 3 agent_session record IDs
    67→And confidence is stored as a float between 0 and 1
    68→
    69→### Scenario: Status transition from pending_approval to active
    70→Given a learning with status "pending_approval" exists
    71→When Tomas approves it
    72→Then status changes to "active"
    73→And approved_by references Tomas's identity
    74→And approved_at is set to current timestamp
    75→
    76→### Scenario: Supersession preserves history
    77→Given active learning A "Use snake_case" exists
    78→When learning B "Use camelCase" supersedes it
    79→Then learning A status changes to "superseded"
    80→And learning A has superseded_by referencing learning B
    81→And learning B has supersedes referencing learning A
    82→And learning B status is "active"
    83→
    84→## Acceptance Criteria
    85→- [ ] `learning` table is SCHEMAFULL with all fields defined and type-constrained
    86→- [ ] `learning_type` asserted IN ["constraint", "instruction", "precedent"]
    87→- [ ] `status` asserted IN ["active", "pending_approval", "dismissed", "superseded", "deactivated"]
    88→- [ ] `source` asserted IN ["human", "agent"]
    89→- [ ] `target_agents` is array of strings, each asserted IN ["code_agent", "chat_agent", "pm_agent", "architect", "observer", "design_partner"]
    90→- [ ] Embedding field supports HNSW index for vector search (duplicate/conflict detection)
    91→- [ ] Workspace, status, and created_at indexes defined for query performance
    92→- [ ] Supersession fields (superseded_by, supersedes) support learning evolution
    93→- [ ] Migration script is versioned and transactional
    94→
    95→## Technical Notes
    96→- Schema follows existing patterns: observation table (text, status, workspace, embedding, source_agent, created_at) and suggestion table (text, status, workspace, embedding, confidence, suggested_by)
    97→- HNSW index on embedding field using DIMENSION 1536 DIST COSINE (same as observation, suggestion)
    98→- Workspace index for feed queries: `DEFINE INDEX learning_workspace_status ON learning FIELDS workspace, status`
    99→- Migration file: `schema/migrations/NNNN_add_learning_table.surql` (next available prefix)
   100→- No data migration needed -- new table, no existing data
   101→- Relation tables: `learning_evidence` TYPE RELATION for evidence edges, `supersedes` TYPE RELATION for supersession
   102→
   103→## Dependencies
   104→- No dependencies -- this is the foundation other stories depend on
   105→- Enables: US-AL-001, US-AL-002, US-AL-003, US-AL-004
   106→

data.command:
Review this conversation for items that were discussed but NOT logged via the Brain MCP tools.

IMPORTANT: If this was a trivial session (greetings, test messages, simple Q&A with no implementation work), immediately respond with:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"No substantive work to log"}
Do NOT explain why there is nothing to log.

Otherwise, check each category:

1. **Decisions**: Architecture or design choices where alternatives existed (e.g. choosing a pattern, library, data model, or trade-off). Do NOT log mechanical bugfixes, typo corrections, or changes with only one correct approach. Call create_provisional_decision for each genuine decision.
2. **Questions**: Unresolved questions raised but not logged. Call ask_question for each.
3. **Observations**: Contradictions, duplication, missing items, deprecated patterns, or anomalies noticed. Call log_observation for each.
4. **Task progress**: Tasks worked on whose status wasn't updated. Call update_task_status for each.
5. **Subtasks identified**: Work items or follow-up tasks discussed but not created. Call create_subtask for each (requires a parent task).
6. **Suggestions**: Optimizations, risks, opportunities, missing elements, or potential pivots noticed during work. Call create_suggestion for each with category (optimization|risk|opportunity|conflict|missing|pivot), rationale, and confidence (0-1).

After logging everything, respond with valid JSON in this exact shape:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"<one-line session summary>","decisions_made":["<decision-id>"],"questions_asked":["<question-id>"],"tasks_progressed":[{"task_id":"<task-id>","from_status":"<from>","to_status":"<to>"}],"files_changed":[{"path":"<path>","change_type":"created|modified|deleted"}],"observations_logged":["<observation-id>"],"subtasks_created":["<subtask-id>"],"suggestions_created":["<suggestion-id>"]}

If you cannot log required items (for example MCP unavailable), respond with:
{"decision":"block","reason":"Log these items first: ..."}

# US-AL-002: Agent Suggests a Behavioral Learning

## Problem
The Observer agent scans the knowledge graph and detects that coding agents have been corrected about the same pattern ("null vs undefined") across 3 separate sessions in 2 weeks. Currently, the Observer can only log observations -- it cannot propose persistent behavioral changes. The same corrections recur because there is no mechanism to convert detected patterns into permanent rules.

## Who
- Observer agent | Detects correction patterns across sessions | Wants to suggest persistent learnings for human approval
- PM agent | Detects workflow anti-patterns | Wants to suggest learnings for other agents (cross-agent coaching)

## Job Story Trace
- **Job 2**: Agent Self-Improvement
- **When** an agent notices it has been corrected on the same pattern three times, **I want to** suggest a behavioral learning, **so I can** prevent future failures without requiring human initiative.

## Solution
Enable agents (Observer, PM, and others) to create learning suggestions with status "pending_approval" that surface in the governance feed for human review. Suggestions include evidence references to the sessions/observations that triggered them.

## Domain Examples

### 1: Happy Path -- Observer suggests learning from correction pattern
The Observer agent's graph scan identifies 3 corrections about "null vs undefined" across sessions on March 5, 8, and 11. It checks existing active learnings -- no match. It creates a learning suggestion with text "Never use null for domain data values. Represent absence with omitted optional fields," confidence 0.89, target_agents ["code_agent"], evidence_refs linking to the 3 sessions, and status "pending_approval." The suggestion appears in Tomas's governance feed as a yellow-tier card.

### 2: Edge Case -- Cross-agent coaching (PM suggests for coding agent)
The PM agent notices that coding agents consistently create tasks without linking them to parent features, causing orphaned work items. The PM creates a learning suggestion with suggested_by "pm_agent", target_agents ["code_agent"], text "When creating tasks, always link to parent feature via belongs_to relation," and confidence 0.78. The suggestion surfaces in the governance feed for Tomas to approve.

### 3: Error/Boundary -- Pattern below threshold
The Observer detects only 2 corrections about "RecordId string format" in the past 14 days -- below the 3-occurrence threshold. Instead of creating a learning suggestion, it logs an observation: "Emerging pattern: RecordId corrections (2 occurrences). Monitoring for recurrence." If a third correction appears, the Observer creates the full suggestion.

## UAT Scenarios (BDD)

### Scenario: Observer creates learning suggestion from correction pattern
Given the Observer agent scans the knowledge graph
And agent_session:a1b2c3 on March 5 contains correction "don't use null"
And agent_session:d4e5f6 on March 8 contains correction "we never use null"
And agent_session:g7h8i9 on March 11 contains correction "null is a contract violation"
When the Observer runs pattern detection with threshold 3
Then a learning record is created with status "pending_approval"
And suggested_by is "observer" and confidence is 0.89
And evidence_refs link to the 3 agent_session records

### Scenario: Observer skips pattern covered by existing learning
Given an active learning "Never use null for domain data values" exists
And 3 corrections about null usage exist in the graph
When the Observer checks for existing coverage via embedding similarity
Then no new suggestion is created
And an observation is logged "Correction pattern matches existing active learning"

### Scenario: Pattern below suggestion threshold
Given only 2 corrections about "RecordId string format" exist in 14 days
When the Observer evaluates whether to suggest a learning
Then no learning suggestion is created
And an info-level observation is logged "Emerging pattern (2 occurrences). Monitoring."

### Scenario: Cross-agent coaching suggestion
Given the PM agent detected coding agents create tasks without feature links
When the PM agent creates a learning suggestion
Then the learning has suggested_by "pm_agent" and target_agents ["code_agent"]
And status is "pending_approval"
And the suggestion surfaces in Tomas's governance feed

### Scenario: Pending suggestion appears in governance feed
Given an Observer learning suggestion with confidence 0.89 exists
When Tomas opens the governance feed
Then a yellow-tier card shows the suggestion text
And displays "Suggested by: Observer | Confidence: 89% | For: code_agent"
And shows evidence quotes from 3 sessions
And has Approve, Edit & Approve, and Dismiss buttons

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Agents can create learning suggestions with status "pending_approval", suggested_by, target_agents, confidence, evidence_refs, and embedding
- [ ] Observer detects correction patterns when 3+ corrections on same topic occur within 14 days
- [ ] Observer checks existing active learnings before suggesting (skips if covered, similarity > 0.80)
- [ ] Suggestions below confidence threshold of 0.70 are not created (logged as observations instead)
- [ ] Cross-agent coaching supported: any agent can suggest learnings for any other agent type
- [ ] Pending suggestions surface as yellow-tier governance feed cards with evidence and approval controls
- [ ] Evidence references are preserved and displayable in the governance feed

## Technical Notes
- Correction pattern detection uses embedding similarity clustering on messages marked as corrections
- Observer already has a graph scanning loop -- learning suggestion is a new output type alongside observations
- Feed card rendering follows existing suggestion card pattern (same visual tier, similar controls)
- Learning suggestion reuses the same `learning` table with different `source` and `status` values (not a separate table)
- Suggestion threshold (3 occurrences, 14-day window, 0.70 confidence) should be configurable per workspace

## Dependencies
- Depends on: US-AL-005 (Learning Schema) -- schema must exist
- Depends on: US-AL-004 (Governance Feed) -- feed cards must render pending learnings
- Depends on: Observer agent existing graph scan infrastructure
- Enables: US-AL-001 implicitly (agent suggestions complement human creation)
